note: seeded defects: low-contrast muted + unlabeled icon button
lift muted contrast to #5b6470; add aria-label to provider button
capture viewport screenshots for the visual record
why: chose viewport over fullPage: a vertically-centred layout produces a tall fullPage image that crops to empty space in the thumbnail

note: marble-ink-jpn background (suminagashi) behind the sign-in card
why: user: background is boring. Resolved a design.md conflict: line 32 says derive the palette from the accent (#4f46e5) but line 24 forbids purple-to-blue gradients. Biased the NEUTRALS toward indigo instead of ramping the accent, so it reads as sumi ink on paper, not a purple hero. Used the global build over the ESM entry because file:// blocks module imports and would fail the console check. Rejected the ember/dusk presets outright. Both land on purple/pink.

note: error state: hanging-rule message, ink stills on error
why: audit critical said the ink 'encodes nothing true'. Bound motion to state via pause() rather than adding a palette shift. Chose stillness over agitation because motion beside text you need someone to READ competes with it. Rejected a filled red banner: design.md spends boldness once and that is already the CTA.

checks.name: a11y: axe-core @375px (contrast, labels, names, roles, target-size)
a11y: axe could not decide @375px (needs human review)

a11y: axe could not decide @1280px (needs human review)

note: prune agentation: remove --annotation, delete design-watch, correct the README trigger claim
why: the trigger was never proven. Zero sessions ever attached to :8000. Kept dead scaffolding implies a capability the project does not have, and the README called it a loop on the strength of it. Chose deletion over keeping it behind a flag: an unproven path that nothing exercises rots silently.

note: measure all four hierarchy levers and store them on the run
why: hierarchy was being judged by the auditor as a whole rather than measured; size ratios and spacing rhythm are numbers, and a number that only prints to stdout cannot catch a regression

craft.note: tabIndex >= 0 and not disabled

craft.note: a held scale is <= 6 steps; more than that is accumulation, not a system

craft.name: size steps are far enough apart to rank
craft.note: 2 step(s) below 1.1x: present in CSS, invisible on screen

craft.name: weight is used as a rank
craft.note: weight differentiates at least two ranks

craft.name: text ranks are separable by contrast
craft.note: every adjacent pair separates

craft.name: spacing follows a rhythm
craft.note: no base unit fits 75% of gaps. Spacing is arbitrary, so proximity groups nothing

note: loading + success states, authored interaction states, hierarchy fixes
why: the audit's standing critical was that no pseudo-class styling existed anywhere. Added hover/focus-visible/active/disabled, each pairing colour with a non-colour cue because colour-only state vanishes in forced-colours mode. Loading is an ATTRIBUTE state on the card, not an element: the button reports its own progress so attention never leaves the control you pressed. And I widened state-matrix to accept that rather than bending the design to fit the checker. Collapsed 13px into 14px (the 12/13 step was 1.077x, invisible on screen) and moved gaps onto a 4px rhythm. Lifted the success green because it sat at the same luminance as the error red, so status was carried by hue alone.

note: copy: drop the em dash; wire verify-render into publish
why: an em dash in UI copy is an AI-writing tell and was standing in for a full stop. Added it to design.md and made it a deterministic check rather than a note, because an unmeasured rule is not a checked rule. Also wired verify-render into the publish step: six consecutive runs had never had craft or states run against them, and every one showed a green gate.

note: all 4 state-matrix states carry a real treatment: loading rides the button, error/locked use a hanging-rule annotation, success stays quiet
why: message 1's intent was to complete design treatments of every declared state; error+locked share one grammar (boldness spent once on the CTA, not a competing red banner), locked adds a disabled-CTA non-colour cue

note: close cold-audit findings on locked: reachable on the real path (fails accumulate, no reset-on-success) and ?state=locked now sets data-locked so the disabled-CTA non-colour cue renders on the review path; respectReducedMotion made explicit on the canvas
why: the cold audit found locked was the one incomplete state (unreachable as-lived, colour-alone as-reviewed); design.md forbids state-by-colour-alone, so the review path must carry the same non-colour cue as the click path

note: close Sonnet cold-audit findings: ink.resume() was dead (no such method; canvas froze permanently after first error) -> ink.play(); loading now reachable via ?state=loading and the workbench dropdown by routing show() through setLoading
why: a model-decorrelated (Sonnet) cold audit caught two bugs both Opus audits missed: a silently-swallowed ink.resume?.() that broke the motion-carries-state intent, and loading being reachable only on the click path despite the file promising ?state=<name> for review
